channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Love connects with Reed on 2 TDs as Packers use fast start to beat Dolphins 30-17
item.description: Jordan Love threw two touchdown passes to Jayden Reed and the Green Bay Packers capitalized on a fast start to beat Miami 30-17 and snap the Dolphins’ three-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Desrosiers scores 3 TDs as Memphis tops No. 18 Tulane 34-24 to end the Green Wave's faint CFP hopes
item.description: Greg Desrosiers Jr. caught two scoring passes and ran for a touchdown, and Memphis defeated No. 18 Tulane 34-24 to knock the Green Wave out of contention for a berth in the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Overshown's flashy pick-6 boosts Cowboys in 27-20 Thanksgiving win over Giants
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — DeMarvion Overshown wasn't sure he could run down the pass he tipped and turn it into an interception for a touchdown.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 49ers QB Brock Purdy remains severely limited by injury to his throwing shoulder
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Brock Purdy threw one short pass in the open portion of practice for the San Francisco 49ers as he remains slowed by an injury to his throwing shoulder that has already forced him to miss a game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bears coach Matt Eberflus explains decision to not call timeout in final seconds as fans clamor for firing
item.description: The Chicago Bears had a chance to tie, or win, their game against the Detroit Lions on Thanksgiving Day — but time expired while they still had a timeout to use.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lions extend win streak to 10 games, hold off Bears for 23-20 victory
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The Detroit Lions have been dominant for much of the season, leading the league in point differential after routinely routing opponents. Against Chicago, the Lions were good enough to extend their winning streak to 10 games. Barely.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Battle, former Tennessee coach and Alabama AD who founded licensing company, dies
item.description: TUSCALOOSA, Ala. (AP) — Bill Battle III, who was athletic director at his alma mater, Alabama, where he played for Paul “Bear” Bryant's first national championship team, then later coached the Tennessee Volunteers and founded the Collegiate Licensing Company, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hall's fifth career hat trick leads Blackhawks over Stars 6-2
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Taylor Hall scored his fifth career hat trick, Connor Bedard, Teuvo Teravainen and Louis Crevier also scored, and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Dallas Stars 6-2 on Wednesday night for their second win in three games. Hall’s game-opening goal 17 seconds in set the tone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Paul-Tyson ring girl shares how fans thought she was part of 'staged' fight after Mike Tyson slap
item.description: Sydney Thomas had to act cool after Mike Tyson slapped Jake Paul, but that led to more speculation from fans saying the fight was all staged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Depleted Jazz lose to Denver, 122-103
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Without injured stars Lauri Markkanen, John Collins and Jordan Clarkson, the Utah Jazz had little chance against the Denver Nuggets and lost for the sixth time in their last seven games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Caitlin Clark is a bigger star than NBA's best players, famed podcast host says
item.description: Caitlin Clark is the face of women's basketball and women's sports, but podcast host Bill Simmons says she's an even bigger star than the NBA's best.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Column: As offense ascends, Bears defense goes on sneaky decline under Matt Eberflus
item.description: The Chicago Bears' defense has been struggling to stop opposing offenses, resulting in a five-game losing streak, while the offense has been unable to score enough points to win games.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bucs with Bowles looks back at dominating win over the Giants
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers returned from their bye week in dominating fashion with a 30-7 win over the New York Giants that ended a four-game losing streak and helped their playoff hopes in the process.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New stadium, new look: Salt Lake Bees unveil 2025 logos, uniforms
item.description: It's a whole new era for the Salt Lake Bees as they prepare for their move to South Jordan where they will play in their brand new home of Daybreak Field.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Russell and Hamilton give Mercedes a 1-2 finish in Las Vegas
item.description: An ecstatic George Russell had just won the Formula 1 race Saturday night when he exclaimed in his radio, “Vegas!” He was ready to start celebrating in America's party capital, saying he would skip his flight back to his home in the United Kingdom to make the most of his Las Vegas Grand Prix victory
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Giannis gets triple-double as Bucks rout Pacers in NBA Cup
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Giannis Antetokounmpo had 37 points, 11 assists, and 10 rebounds for his second triple-double in his last four games, and the Milwaukee Bucks beat the Indiana Pacers 129-117 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Purdy, Bosa out for Green Bay matchup, Allen steps up as QB
item.description: (KRON) – San Francisco 49ers head coach Kyle Shanahan announced in a press conference Friday that quarterback Brock Purdy will be out for Sunday’s game against the Green Bay Packers. Purdy injured his right shoulder in last Sunday's loss to the Seattle Seahawks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: 2024 F1 Las Vegas Grand Prix preview: Racing down the Strip
item.description: The 2024 Formula 1 World Championship heads to Las Vegas this weekend for what will be the third to last race of the season.
item.dc:creator: Viknesh Vijayenthiran - Motor Authority, Fox 5
